mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 9.5% triggers a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 9.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 650,000 units signals a weakening in residential investment and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 650,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 102.5 signals a strengthening in supply chain capacity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 102.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% triggers a monetary easing cycle that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 13,250 signals a strengthening in aggregate demand and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 13,250 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening in labor market conditions and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the consumer price index above 220 signals a strengthening in inflation expectations and monetary policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 220 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
